sanskrit: प्रकृतिं स्वामवष्टभ्य विसृजामि पुनः पुनः । भूतग्राममिमं कृत्स्नमवशं प्रकृतेर्वशात् ॥
hindiTranslation: प्रकृतिके वशमें होनेसे परतन्त्र हुए इस सम्पूर्ण प्राणिसमुदायको मैं कल्पोंके आदिमें अपनी प्रकृतिको वशमें करके बारबार रचता हूँ ॥
englishTranslation: Taking hold of my own nature, I repeatedly create this entire multitude of beings, helpless under the sway of nature.
essence: Life unfolds through a power larger than individual will.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Krishna says he keeps bringing forth the whole world again and again through his own nature, while beings remain bound by nature’s power.
simpleInsight: The world is not random. A deeper order is moving through every cycle of creation.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen between duty and grief. Krishna widens the lens: creation itself moves in cycles, and beings are carried by nature’s force. This verse explains how the world keeps unfolding under a power greater than individual control.
modernRelevance: You watch the same family pattern repeat in a new generation and feel helpless to stop it. This verse points to a larger force shaping the cycle, even when you cannot yet see it clearly.
translationLiteral: प्रकृतिम् (nature) / स्वाम् (my own) / अवष्टभ्य (taking hold of) / विसृजामि (I create, send forth) / पुनः पुनः (again and again) / भूतग्रामम् (multitude of beings) / इमम् (this) / कृत्स्नम् (entire) / अवशम् (helpless, not independent) / प्रकृतेः (of nature) / वशात् (under the sway)
